# Generic-by-default harness adapter surface â€” minimize per-family (Claude / Codex / Antigravity / â€¦) logic

## Observation (operator-surfaced)

The harness carries a growing amount of **per-family** logic â€” Claude-specific (e.g. `.claude/hooks/laneStateStopHook.mjs`, `.claude/` settings) and Codex-specific adapters. A NEW family (**Antigravity**, and any future one) has **none** yet â†’ onboarding a new family currently means writing a new per-family logic fork, not dropping in a thin adapter.

## Question

Should the harness logic be **mostly generic** (family-agnostic core + thin per-family adapters), so a new family plugs in via a small adapter instead of a logic-fork?

## The pattern already exists â€” extend it

`ai/scripts/lifecycle/stopHookDecision.mjs` is the model: *"Shared pure decision primitives for lane-state Stop hooks. Harness adapters own payload extraction and transport behavior; this module owns the no-hold decision semantics so Claude and Codex cannot drift."* The generic core (pure decision) + thin per-harness adapter (payload/transport) is the right shape. The just-merged deference + hold-lexicon work (#13740) lives in that shared core â€” good.

## Direction (design surface â€” may graduate to ideation)

- Audit where per-family logic lives vs the generic core (hooks, settings, identity binding, transport).
- Extract family-agnostic decision/semantics into shared modules; keep ONLY payload-extraction + transport in per-family adapters.
- Define the adapter contract so **Antigravity** (the forcing function) onboards via a thin adapter â€” no logic fork.
- Net: N families Ă— thin adapters, not N Ă— logic forks.

## Out of scope
- The shared decision semantics themselves (those stay generic by definition).

Surfaced by @tobiu; filed by @neo-opus-ada as the follow-up to the open-PR sweep.

## Ticket Intake Refresh â€” `needs-contract-alignment` inside `needs-design`

V-B-A refresh on 2026-06-23:

- Live issue body has direction only; no Contract Ledger is present.
- Current substrate already has partial generic seams: `ai/scripts/lifecycle/stopHookDecision.mjs` owns shared stop-hook semantics, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/harnessRouting.mjs` owns family-to-target defaults, and `ai/scripts/lifecycle/resumeHarness.mjs` selects concrete adapters.
- Per-harness adapters still carry real payload/transport differences: `.codex/hooks/codex-lane-state-stop.mjs`, `.claude/hooks/laneStateStopHook.mjs`, `antigravity-cli`, `codex-app-server`, and `osascript` paths are not interchangeable implementation details.
- Successor sweep found prior partial primitives (#10604, #10677, #13015, #10517), but no newer issue/PR superseding #13796.

[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]

**Verdict:** `needs-narrowing`; keep `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`.

The direction is valid, but the next step is not code. Because this proposes a consumed adapter surface for agents/harnesses, it needs a Contract Ledger before implementation. Minimal rows I would expect:

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| shared harness adapter contract | #13796 + current `harnessRouting`/`resumeHarness` seams | define the family-agnostic inputs/outputs each adapter must satisfy | unknown/unsupported family fails closed with a clear diagnostic; no silent fallback to the wrong host | JSDoc + harness workflow docs | unit tests with fake Claude/Codex/Antigravity targets |
| stop-hook payload extraction boundary | current Codex + Claude hook adapters | shared semantics stay in `stopHookDecision`; adapters own only transcript/payload extraction and host transport | malformed hook payload fails open/audits, never traps a session | hook JSDoc | existing/new stop-hook adapter tests |
| fresh-session resume transport boundary | `resumeHarness` + `harnessRouting` | adapters expose capability metadata: CLI/app-server/osascript, live-host gate, fresh-session behavior | live-host operations stay opt-in/gated; unsupported capability refuses before side effects | `agent-harness.md` or lifecycle docs | mock adapter tests; live proof remains operator-gated |
| identity/route binding | `identityRoots` + `harnessRouting` | derive routing from AgentIdentity model family and explicit metadata, not duplicated handle maps | benched/unknown identities do not receive accidental routes | docs/JSDoc | identity-routing unit coverage |

That ledger should also split the first implementation into one narrow slice. A good first slice is probably a read-only contract/test extraction around `harnessRouting` + `resumeHarness` adapter metadata, not a broad rewrite of every hook and wake path.
